lyrics.english:
Namami Yamunam aham sakala siddhi hetum muda.
Murari pada pankaja sphurad amanda renutkatam. (1)

translation.english: I bow to Yamuna, the bestower of all spiritual attainments, enriched by the dust of Krishna’s lotus feet.

lyrics.english:
Kalinda giri mastake patad amanda purojjvalam.
Vilasa gamanollasat prakat ganda shailonnatam. (2)

translation.english: Flowing from Mount Kalinda, she shines brilliantly and moves with divine playfulness.

lyrics.english:
Sagosha ghosha kallola taranga bhanga rajitam.
Dvisat surasurendra bhaya rakshana pradam shubham. (3)

translation.english: Her waves resound beautifully; she protects devotees and removes fear from all beings.

lyrics.english:
Tatastha nava kanana prakat moda pushpambuna.
Surasura supujita smita mukhi sada shubha. (4)

translation.english: Her banks are adorned with fresh forests and flowers; she is worshipped by all and radiates joy.

lyrics.english:
Jala sthita navina mina makaranda mugdha ikshanam.
Vrajesha tanaya priyam atula bhagya dam shubham. (5)

translation.english: Filled with fish and beauty, she is dear to Krishna and grants unmatched fortune.

lyrics.sanskrit:
मधुराधिपतेः सदा प्रियतमा शुभां भजेत् ।
भवाब्धितरणे सदा भवति सा सहायिनी ॥६॥
lyrics.english:
Madhuradhipateh sada priyatama shubham bhajet.
Bhavabdhi tarane sada bhavati sa sahayini. (6)

translation.english: Beloved of Krishna, she helps devotees cross the ocean of worldly existence.

lyrics.sanskrit:
भवभयविनाशिनीं सकलसिद्धिदां शुभाम् ।
श्रितजनसुखप्रदां नमति यः स मुच्यते ॥७॥
lyrics.english:
Bhava bhaya vinashinim sakala siddhi dam shubham.
Shrita jana sukha pradam namati yah sa muchyate. (7)

translation.english: She destroys worldly fear and grants blessings; those who bow to her are liberated.

lyrics.sanskrit:
इति स्तवमिमं सदा पठति यो नरः श्रद्धया ।
स याति परमां गतिं हरिपदं लभेत् ध्रुवम् ॥८॥
lyrics.english:
Iti stavam imam sada pathati yo narah shraddhaya.
Sa yati paramam gatim hari padam labhet dhruvam. (8)

translation.english: One who recites this with devotion attains the highest state and reaches Krishna.
